metadata.title: Important Days and Events Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: What is the theme for International Nurses Day 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: Our Nurses. Our Future. Caring for nurses strengthens economies

options.label: B
options.text: Nurses: A Voice to Lead – Nursing the World to Health

options.label: C
options.text: Nurses: A Voice to Lead – A Vision for Future Healthcare

answer.label: A
answer.text: Our Nurses. Our Future. Caring for nurses strengthens economies

questions.explanation: International Nurses Day, observed annually on May 12th, the birth anniversary of Florence Nightingale, recognizes the vital contributions of nurses worldwide. The theme for International Nurses Day 2025 is “Our Nurses. Our Future. Caring for nurses strengthens economies”. This year’s theme emphasizes the economic and societal importance of investing in nurses, their well-being, and their rights.

questions.question: The Indian government has officially designated which day as Ayurveda Day?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Government of India declared 23rd September as the new fixed date for celebrating Ayurveda Day each year. This decision replaces the earlier variable observance on Dhanteras, which followed the lunar calendar and created logistical challenges. The change was announced through a Gazette Notification dated 23rd March 2025 to ensure global visibility and consistent annual observance. Ayurveda Day promotes Ayurveda as a scientific and holistic system of preventive healthcare. The date 23rd September was chosen as it coincides with the autumnal equinox, symbolizing natural balance, aligning with Ayurveda’s core philosophy of harmony between mind, body, and spirit.

questions.question: What is the theme for International Museum Day 2025?

options.label: A
options.text: The Future of Museums in Rapidly Changing Communities

options.label: D
options.text: The Future of Museums: Recover and Reimagine

answer.label: A
answer.text: The Future of Museums in Rapidly Changing Communities

questions.explanation: May 18 is celebrated as International Museum Day every year to show how museums help us connect with history, culture, science, and art. Museums are more than just places to keep old things. They are learning spaces that help us understand the past and imagine the future. People are encouraged to visit museums on this day and explore the knowledge they offer. The theme for International Museum Day 2025 is “The Future of Museums in Rapidly Changing Communities.” It highlights the need for museums to adapt to changes in technology, climate, population, and lifestyles. This year’s theme calls on museums to become more digital, more inclusive, and more connected to their local communities.

questions.question: Which country recently hosted the Second Blue Talks to promote global marine conservation efforts?
questions.explanation: India recently hosted the Second Blue Talks in New Delhi to promote global marine conservation efforts. The event was organized by the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES) in collaboration with the Embassies of France and Costa Rica. It aimed to build momentum ahead of the 3rd United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC3) to be held in Nice, France. Blue Talks is a multilateral consultation platform that connects governments, scientists, and civil society. It focuses on sustainable ocean use and supports Sustainable Development Goal 14 (SDG 14 – Life Below Water). The event promoted marine research, education, and the launch of strategic knowledge tools.

questions.question: Which day is celebrated as World Dugong Day every year ?
questions.explanation: May 28th is celebrated every year as World Dugong Day, highlighting the need to protect dugongs in marine ecosystems. Dugongs, scientifically called Dugong dugon, are the only herbivorous marine mammals found in India. They are often called sea cows and look like a mix between a seal and a whale. Dugongs are found in warm coastal waters of India including the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Gulf of Mannar, Palk Bay, and Gulf of Kutch. They depend on seagrass beds like Cymodocea, Halophila, Thalassia, and Halodule for food. Dugongs can live up to 70 years and are listed as Vulnerable on the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) Red List.

questions.question: Which city is the host of India’s first Agriculture Hackathon?
questions.explanation: India’s First Agricultural Hackathon was inaugurated at Sinchan Nagar Grounds, College of Agriculture, Pune, Maharashtra. It is jointly organised by the State Agriculture Department, Mahatma Phule Krishi Vidyapeeth (MPKV), and College of Agriculture, Pune. The event highlights the shared responsibility of farmers, industries, scientists, government, and society in improving agriculture.

questions.question: Which country is the host of 12th BRICS parliamentary forum in 2026?
questions.explanation: The 11th BRICS Parliamentary Forum concluded in Brasilia, Brazil, on June 5 with strong condemnation of the recent terrorist attack in Pahalgam, India. The Indian delegation was led by Lok Sabha Speaker and included senior parliamentarians and officials. The forum included 10 member countries—India, Brazil, Russia, China, South Africa, Iran, United Arab Emirates (UAE), Egypt, Ethiopia, and Indonesia. India was given the chairmanship to host the 12th BRICS Parliamentary Forum in 2026.

questions.question: Where is the World Expo 2025 being held?
questions.explanation: The India Pavilion at World Expo 2025 in Osaka, Japan, has been ranked among the top five pavilions along with the United States, Italy, Japan, and France. It highlights India’s rich cultural heritage, innovation, and sustainable development in a vibrant and engaging way. The pavilion includes sections on heritage, Ayurveda, Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), and more. It has been curated by the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA). The World Expo will continue till 13th October 2025.

questions.question: Where was the 137th meeting of the Offshore Security Coordination Committee (OSCC) held?
questions.explanation: The Indian Coast Guard (ICG) recently chaired the 137th meeting of the Offshore Security Coordination Committee (OSCC) in New Delhi. The committee frames policies, prepares contingency plans, and guides multiple agencies in securing offshore installations. Due to the growing offshore Oil and Natural Gas sector, the Indian Coast Guard’s role has expanded significantly. Indian Coast Guard (ICG), formed under the Coast Guard Act, 1978. It is an armed force under the Ministry of Defence.

questions.question: World Population Day is observed every year on which day?
questions.explanation: World Population Day is observed every year on 11 July to raise awareness about global population issues. It was established by the United Nations (UN) in 1989. The idea came from the milestone when the world’s population reached 5 billion on 11 July 1987. This year the theme is “Empowering young people to create the families they want in a fair and hopeful world“.
